userStories.title: Initialize anacare_product repo structure
userStories.description: Set up the directory skeleton for the consumer platform in /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/. Create these directories: /backend/ (FastAPI app), /frontend/ (React Vite app), /tools/ (ingestion scripts), /schema/ (SQL migrations). Copy /Users/joshuakao/anacare_03.20/tools/schema.sql into /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/schema/001_base.sql. Create .env.example at repo root with variables: DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, AWS_REGION, S3_BUCKET, TARGET_CPT_CODES. Create requirements.txt at root with: fastapi, uvicorn[standard], psycopg2-binary, ijson, requests, boto3, python-dotenv.
acceptanceCriteria: Directories /backend/, /frontend/, /tools/, /schema/ exist in /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/
.env.example lists all 8 required env vars
schema/001_base.sql contains the base schema copied verbatim from anacare_03.20/tools/schema.sql
requirements.txt lists all 7 packages

userStories.title: Extend DB schema with consumer platform tables
acceptanceCriteria: schema/002_consumer_platform.sql exists in /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/schema/
All 6 tables are defined: hospitals, hospital_quality, affiliations, surgeon_volume, plans, zipcodes
PostGIS extension is created and spatial indexes are defined on hospitals.geom and zipcodes.geom
File runs against a fresh PostgreSQL 15+ instance without errors

userStories.title: Ingest CMS Hospital General Information into hospitals table
userStories.description: Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/ingest_hospitals.py. Download CMS Hospital General Information (dataset xubh-q36u) by paginating the CMS data API: GET https://data.cms.gov/provider-data/api/1/datastore/query/xubh-q36u/0?limit=5000&offset=0, incrementing offset by 5000 until empty results. Extract per record: provider_ccn -> ccn, hospital_name -> name, city, state, zip_code -> zip (first 5 chars), hospital_overall_rating -> cms_star_rating (cast to float; set NULL if value is 'Not Available'). Upsert into hospitals table using ON CONFLICT (ccn) DO UPDATE SET name=EXCLUDED.name, city=EXCLUDED.city, state=EXCLUDED.state, zip=EXCLUDED.zip, cms_star_rating=EXCLUDED.cms_star_rating. After insert, populate lat/lng from zipcodes table: UPDATE hospitals h SET lat=z.lat, lng=z.lng FROM zipcodes z WHERE h.zip=z.zip AND h.lat IS NULL. Then update geom: UPDATE hospitals SET geom=ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(lng::float, lat::float), 4326) WHERE lat IS NOT NULL AND geom IS NULL. DB connection from env vars DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_NAME, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD. Print on completion: 'Inserted/updated N hospitals, M had no zip match.'
acceptanceCriteria: tools/ingest_hospitals.py exists in /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/
Script paginates CMS API until all records are fetched (expect 5000+ hospitals)
cms_star_rating is NULL for 'Not Available' values, not 0 or a string
Records upsert without duplicate key errors on re-run
Script prints final count of inserted and zip-unmatched records

userStories.title: Ingest CMS Hospital Complications, Deaths, and Readmissions into hospital_quality
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/ingest_quality.py. Run two downloads in a single script:

1. CMS Hospital Complications and Deaths (dataset yc9t-dgbk): paginate GET https://data.cms.gov/provider-data/api/1/datastore/query/yc9t-dgbk/0?limit=5000&offset=0. For each record extract: facility_id -> ccn, measure_id, score (float or NULL if 'Not Available'/'N/A'/''), compared_to_national. Filter to measure_ids: COMP_HIP_KNEE, PSI_11, MORT_30_CABG, Hybrid_HWM. Upsert into hospital_quality (ccn, measure_id, score, compared_to_national, year=2024).

2. CMS Hospital Readmissions (dataset 9n3s-kdb3): paginate same API pattern. Filter to measure_id READM_30_HOSP_WIDE. Upsert into hospital_quality with year=2024.

Skip any record where ccn is not found in the hospitals table (foreign key safety). Print on completion: 'Complications: N records. Readmissions: M records. Skipped P (unknown CCN).'
acceptanceCriteria: tools/ingest_quality.py exists in /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/
COMP_HIP_KNEE records exist in hospital_quality after run
READM_30_HOSP_WIDE records exist in hospital_quality after run
score is NULL (not 0) for hospitals with 'Not Available' in CMS data
Records with unknown CCN are skipped, not inserted
Script does not crash on malformed or missing score values

userStories.title: Ingest zip code centroids into zipcodes table
userStories.description: Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/ingest_zipcodes.py. Use the SimpleMaps free US zip code database. Download the free CSV from https://simplemaps.com/static/data/us-zips/1.82/basic/simplemaps_uszips_basicv1.82.zip (or the current version link — check the page if this URL 404s). Unzip and parse uszips.csv. Columns to extract: zip, lat, lng, city, state_id (2-letter) -> state. Insert all ~33K rows into zipcodes table using ON CONFLICT (zip) DO UPDATE SET lat=EXCLUDED.lat, lng=EXCLUDED.lng. After insert, update geom column: UPDATE zipcodes SET geom=ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(lng::float, lat::float), 4326). Then backfill hospitals that have a zip but no lat/lng: UPDATE hospitals h SET lat=z.lat, lng=z.lng, geom=ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(z.lng::float, z.lat::float), 4326) FROM zipcodes z WHERE h.zip=z.zip AND h.lat IS NULL. Print: 'Loaded N zip codes. Backfilled M hospital coordinates.'
acceptanceCriteria: tools/ingest_zipcodes.py exists in /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/
All ~33K US zip codes are loaded into the zipcodes table
geom column is populated for all zipcodes rows after run
Hospitals missing lat/lng are backfilled from zip centroids
Script handles download + unzip automatically without manual steps

userStories.title: Ingest RAND hospital price multipliers
userStories.description: Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/ingest_rand.py. Accept a required --file argument: the local path to the RAND RRA1144-2 supplemental hospital-level CSV (this file must be manually downloaded from rand.org/pubs/research_reports/RRA1144-2.html as it requires accepting terms). Parse the CSV. Look for columns containing 'price' and 'ratio' or 'multiplier' — inspect the actual header to find the commercial-to-Medicare ratio column. Also extract hospital name and state columns. Match each RAND row to the hospitals table using: (1) exact name match lowercased with punctuation stripped, (2) if no exact match, try removing common suffixes ('Hospital', 'Medical Center', 'Health System', 'LLC') and match on name fragment + state. Update hospitals.rand_multiplier for matched rows. For any hospital not in RAND dataset, the API layer will use the national average fallback of 2.56 (do NOT update those rows — leave rand_multiplier NULL). Print: 'Matched N of M RAND hospitals. Unmatched: P.'
acceptanceCriteria: tools/ingest_rand.py exists with required --file argument
Parses hospital name and price ratio from the RAND CSV
Updates hospitals.rand_multiplier for matched records
Unmatched hospitals are left with rand_multiplier=NULL (not set to 2.56)
Script prints matched vs unmatched count
Does not crash if hospital name has no match — skips gracefully

userStories.title: Ingest NPPES NPI Registry — surgical providers into providers table
userStories.description: Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/ingest_nppes.py. Accept optional --file argument for a pre-downloaded NPPES zip. If --file not provided, fetch the download page at https://download.cms.gov/nppes/NPI_Files.html, parse it to find the most recent 'Full Replacement Monthly NPI File' ZIP link, and download it (~8GB). Unzip and stream-parse the CSV using csv.reader in chunks of 10000 rows — do NOT load full file into memory. Filter rows where: Entity_Type_Code == '1' (individual) AND Provider_Taxonomy_Code_1 is in this set: 207X00000X (orthopedic), 208G00000X (cardiac surgery), 208600000X (general surgery), 207V00000X (OB/GYN), 208800000X (urology), 207T00000X (neurosurgery), 207RG0100X (gastroenterology), 207L00000X (anesthesiology), 207W00000X (ophthalmology). Skip rows where NPI_Deactivation_Date is non-empty. Extract: NPI -> npi, concat(Provider_Last_Name_Legal_Name + ', ' + Provider_First_Name) -> name, Provider_Credential_Text -> credentials, Provider_Taxonomy_Code_1 -> taxonomy_code, Provider_Business_Practice_Location_Address_City_Name -> city, Provider_Business_Practice_Location_Address_State_Name -> state, first 5 chars of Provider_Business_Practice_Location_Address_Postal_Code -> zip, NPI_Enumeration_Date -> npi_enum_date. First run ALTER TABLE providers ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS credentials TEXT; and ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS npi_enum_date DATE; if not present. Upsert into providers. Print progress every 500K rows. Print final: 'Loaded N surgical providers.'
acceptanceCriteria: tools/ingest_nppes.py exists with optional --file argument
Streams CSV without loading full 8GB into memory (uses chunked csv.reader)
Filters to 9 surgical taxonomy codes only, skips deactivated NPIs
Upserts into providers with name, credentials, taxonomy_code, city, state, zip, npi_enum_date
credentials and npi_enum_date columns are added to providers table if missing
Prints progress every 500K rows and final loaded count

userStories.title: Ingest Medicare Part B surgeon volume into surgeon_volume table
userStories.description: Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/ingest_part_b.py. Accept optional --file argument for a pre-downloaded CSV. If not provided, attempt to download from the CMS data portal: https://data.cms.gov/provider-summary-by-type-of-service/medicare-physician-other-practitioners/medicare-physician-other-practitioners-by-provider-and-service (get the most recent year's CSV download link from that page). The file is ~2.5GB. Stream-parse with csv.reader in 10000-row chunks. Key columns: Rndrng_NPI -> npi, HCPCS_Cd -> cpt_code, Tot_Srvcs -> annual_volume (integer). Filter to only these CPT codes: 27447, 27130, 27407, 23412, 45378, 63030, 58150, 47562, 93455, 73721. For each (NPI, CPT) pair, sum Tot_Srvcs across all rows (there may be multiple rows per NPI+CPT). Accumulate sums in a dict, then batch-upsert into surgeon_volume (npi, cpt_code, annual_volume, year=2023) using ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET annual_volume=EXCLUDED.annual_volume. After load, delete surgeon_volume rows where npi NOT IN (SELECT npi FROM providers) — orphan cleanup. Print: 'Loaded N surgeon_volume records. Deleted M orphan rows.'
acceptanceCriteria: tools/ingest_part_b.py exists with optional --file argument
Streams CSV without loading full 2.5GB file into memory
Filters to 10 target CPT codes only
Aggregates annual_volume correctly across multiple rows for same NPI+CPT
Orphan NPI rows (not in providers) are deleted after load
Prints final count of loaded records and deleted orphans

userStories.title: Ingest CMS Facility Affiliation and city-state fallback into affiliations table
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/ingest_affiliations.py. Two phases:

Phase 1 — CMS Facility Affiliation (dataset 27ea-46a8): paginate GET https://data.cms.gov/provider-characteristics/hospitals-and-other-facilities/facility-affiliation-data/data?limit=5000&offset=0. Each row maps a surgeon NPI to a facility CCN. Column names may be 'ind_pac_id' or 'NPI' for the NPI and 'Facility_CCN' or similar for the CCN — inspect the first row to detect column names. Filter: only insert if npi exists in providers table AND ccn exists in hospitals table. Insert into affiliations (npi, ccn, source='cms_affiliation') using ON CONFLICT (npi, ccn) DO NOTHING. Print: 'Phase 1: N CMS affiliations inserted.'

Phase 2 — City/State Fallback: find all NPIs in providers that have zero rows in affiliations. For each such NPI, find hospitals where hospitals.city = providers.city AND hospitals.state = providers.state. Insert up to 3 best matches per NPI into affiliations (npi, ccn, source='city_state_fallback') using ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. Print: 'Phase 2: M fallback affiliations inserted for P unaffiliated surgeons.'
acceptanceCriteria: tools/ingest_affiliations.py exists in /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/
Phase 1 inserts CMS affiliations with source='cms_affiliation'
Phase 2 inserts fallback affiliations with source='city_state_fallback' for unaffiliated NPIs only
No affiliations reference NPIs or CCNs that do not exist in their respective tables
Max 3 fallback affiliations per NPI
Script is idempotent — re-running does not create duplicate rows

userStories.title: Ingest HealthCare.gov 2026 plan landscape into plans table
userStories.description: Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/ingest_plans.py. Download the HealthCare.gov 2026 individual market plan landscape file. The file index is at https://www.healthcare.gov/health-and-dental-plan-datasets-for-researchers-and-issuers/ — find the 2026 individual market medical plan landscape CSV (typically named plan_landscape_individual_market_medical_2026.csv or similar). Parse it with csv.DictReader. Map columns: 'Plan ID (Standard Component)' -> plan_id, 'Plan Marketing Name' -> plan_name, 'Issuer Name' -> payer, 'Metal Level' -> metal_tier, 'Plan Type' -> network_type, 'State Code' -> state, 'Individual Medical Deductible (In-Network, Standard)' -> deductible_ind, 'Family Medical Deductible (In-Network, Standard)' -> deductible_fam, 'Individual Medical MOOP (In-Network, Standard)' -> oop_max_ind, 'Family Medical MOOP (In-Network, Standard)' -> oop_max_fam. Strip '$' and ',' from all dollar values before casting to float. Set NULL for blank or 'N/A' values. coinsurance_pct and copay columns are not in this file — set them NULL for now. Upsert into plans table using ON CONFLICT (plan_id) DO UPDATE. Print: 'Loaded N plans across S states.'
acceptanceCriteria: tools/ingest_plans.py exists in /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/
Downloads the 2026 individual market plan landscape CSV programmatically
Dollar values are stored as numeric with $ and comma stripped
NULL stored for blank or N/A values (not 0)
Plans are upserted without duplicate key errors on re-run
Prints plan count and state count on completion

userStories.title: Adapt Aetna MRF ingestion pipeline into anacare_product
userStories.description:
Copy and adapt the three MRF scripts from /Users/joshuakao/anacare_03.20/tools/ into /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/ with these modifications:

fetch_mrf_index.py: expand TARGET_CPT_CODES to include all 10 launch CPTs: 27447, 27130, 27407, 23412, 45378, 63030, 58150, 47562, 93455, 73721. Output filtered URLs to .tmp/aetna_mrf_links.json. No other changes.

stream_mrf_file.py: after extracting a rate record, if the provider NPI does not exist in the providers table, insert a minimal stub: INSERT INTO providers (npi, npi_resolved) VALUES (%s, FALSE) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. This ensures rate records can be inserted even before NPPES ingestion. All other logic unchanged.

load_rates_db.py: load from .tmp/raw_rates.jsonl into the rates table. For each record where a hospital CCN can be found via affiliations (npi -> affiliations -> ccn), log it as matched. Upsert with ON CONFLICT (npi, cpt_code, rate_type, billing_class) DO UPDATE SET rate=EXCLUDED.rate, ingested_at=NOW().

Create run_mrf_pipeline.sh in /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/ that runs all three scripts sequentially: python tools/fetch_mrf_index.py && python tools/stream_mrf_file.py --links .tmp/aetna_mrf_links.json && python tools/load_rates_db.py. Add set -e at top so it exits on first failure.
acceptanceCriteria: All 3 MRF scripts exist in /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/
TARGET_CPT_CODES includes all 10 launch CPT codes
stream_mrf_file.py inserts NPI stubs for unknown providers rather than crashing
run_mrf_pipeline.sh runs all 3 steps in sequence with set -e
After pipeline run against a real or test MRF file, rates table has records for CPT 27447

userStories.title: Build EPISODE_COSTS data structure for all 10 launch procedures
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/episode_costs.py. Define a module-level dict EPISODE_COSTS with entries for all 10 procedures. Each entry must follow this exact schema:

{ 'proc_id': str, 'display_name': str, 'cpt_primary': str, 'complication_risk_field': str, 'complication_cost_avg': int, 'implant_cost': int (0 if no implant), 'preop': [ {'name': str, 'cpt': str, 'cost': int, 'visits': int} ], 'postop': [ {'name': str, 'cpt'|'hcpcs': str, 'cost': int, 'visits'|'sessions'|'fills': int, 'cost_per_session': int (PT only), 'rx_tier': int (Rx only)} ] }

Knee Replacement (27447): preop=[surgeon consult 99214 $280 x1, knee MRI 73721 $850 x1, metabolic panel 80053 $120 x1, CBC 85025 $45 x1, EKG 93000 $85 x1, chest X-ray 71046 $65 x1, anesthesia consult 99213 $180 x1], implant_cost=6500, PT sessions=20 at $155/session (97110), post-op X-ray 73560 x2 $120, knee brace L1820 $95, crutches E0110 $65, Rx: oxycodone tier1 $45, xarelto tier2 $35, celecoxib tier1 $25. complication_risk_field=COMP_HIP_KNEE, complication_cost_avg=12000.

Hip Replacement (27130): same preop as knee but use hip X-ray 73502 instead of knee MRI, implant_cost=8500, PT sessions=18, same Rx. complication_risk_field=COMP_HIP_KNEE.

ACL Reconstruction (27407): preop=[surgeon consult, knee MRI 73721, labs], no implant, PT sessions=24, Rx: oxycodone tier1, ibuprofen tier1 $10. complication_risk_field=PSI_11.

Rotator Cuff Repair (23412): preop=[surgeon consult, shoulder MRI 73221 $750, labs], no implant, PT sessions=16. complication_risk_field=PSI_11.

Colonoscopy (45378): preop=[labs 80053+85025 only], no implant, no PT, Rx: bowel prep PEG $35 tier1. complication_risk_field=PSI_11, complication_cost_avg=3500.

Lumbar Discectomy (63030): preop=[surgeon consult, spine MRI 72148 $950, labs, EKG], no implant, PT sessions=12. complication_risk_field=PSI_11.

Hysterectomy (58150): preop=[surgeon consult, pelvic ultrasound 76856 $350, labs], no implant, PT sessions=8. complication_risk_field=PSI_11.

Cholecystectomy (47562): preop=[surgeon consult, abdominal ultrasound 76700 $280, labs], no implant, no PT, Rx: oxycodone tier1. complication_risk_field=PSI_11.

Cardiac Catheterization (93455): preop=[surgeon consult, EKG, chest X-ray, labs, stress test 93017 $350], no implant, no PT, Rx: aspirin tier1 $5, metoprolol tier1 $10, atorvastatin tier1 $12. complication_risk_field=MORT_30_CABG, complication_cost_avg=25000.

MRI Knee (73721): preop=[], implant_cost=0, postop=[], complication_risk_field=None, complication_cost_avg=0. This is a standalone diagnostic — no surgery decomposition.
acceptanceCriteria: tools/episode_costs.py exists in /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/
EPISODE_COSTS dict contains exactly 10 keys matching proc_ids above
Every entry has preop list, implant_cost, postop list, complication_risk_field, complication_cost_avg
All cost values are integers or floats — no strings like '$280'
PT session counts are: knee=20, hip=18, ACL=24, shoulder=16, spine=12, hysterectomy=8
mri_knee entry has empty preop and postop lists (single-phase diagnostic only)

userStories.title: Build multi-phase episode OOP calculation engine
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/compute_episode_oop.py. Implement function compute_episode_oop(episode, negotiated_rate, plan, deductible_remaining, oop_max_remaining, include_complication_risk=False, hospital_complication_rate=None) -> dict.

The episode arg is one EPISODE_COSTS entry. The plan arg is a dict with keys: coinsurance_pct, rx_tier1, rx_tier2, rx_tier3.

Logic — maintain two running trackers across all phases: deductible_consumed (starts at 0) and oop_consumed (starts at 0). For each line item in sequence (preop items, then surgery, then postop items):
- Compute item gross cost (cost * visits, or cost_per_session * sessions for PT, or cost * fills for Rx)
- For Rx items: patient pays plan['rx_tier{N}'] * fills (flat copay, not coinsurance). Add copay to oop_consumed.
- For all other items: if deductible_remaining - deductible_consumed > 0, patient first pays min(gross_cost, deductible_remaining - deductible_consumed) toward deductible, then coinsurance_pct * remainder. Add total to oop_consumed.
- Cap each item's your_cost so that oop_consumed never exceeds oop_max_remaining. If oop_consumed already equals oop_max_remaining, your_cost = 0 for all remaining items.
- Surgery item: gross_cost = negotiated_rate. Decompose for display as: facility=55%, surgeon=30%, anesthesia=18% of surgeon portion, implant from episode['implant_cost'].

If include_complication_risk=True and hospital_complication_rate is not None: add a complication line to postop_items: gross_cost = episode['complication_cost_avg'], apply OOP formula, name='Expected complication cost'.

Return dict: { preop_oop, surgery_oop, postop_oop, total_episode_oop, oop_max_hit (bool), preop_items: [{name, gross_cost, your_cost}], surgery_items: [{name, gross_cost, your_cost}], postop_items: [{name, gross_cost, your_cost}], complication_scenario_oop (float or None) }.

Add __main__ smoke test: knee_replacement, negotiated_rate=18400, plan={coinsurance_pct:0.20, rx_tier1:10, rx_tier2:35, rx_tier3:70}, deductible_remaining=1400, oop_max_remaining=4600. Print all returned values.
acceptanceCriteria: tools/compute_episode_oop.py exists in /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/
Running deductible and OOP trackers carry across all 3 phases — they are not reset between phases
Rx items use rx_tier copay (not coinsurance * cost)
once oop_consumed reaches oop_max_remaining, all remaining items return your_cost=0
Surgery gross_cost decomposes into facility/surgeon/anesthesia/implant for display
Smoke test in __main__ runs without error and prints plausible dollar amounts

userStories.title: Build FastAPI backend with all required endpoints
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/backend/main.py and /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/backend/db.py. Copy db.py from /Users/joshuakao/anacare_03.20/api/db.py and update import paths. Use FastAPI with CORSMiddleware allowing all origins. Implement these endpoints:

GET /health — return {status: 'ok', db: 'connected'} (test DB with a simple SELECT 1).

GET /zipcodes/{zip} — query zipcodes table, return {zip, city, state, lat, lng} or 404.

GET /plans — query plans table, return list of all plans as [{plan_id, plan_name, payer, metal_tier, network_type, state}] sorted by payer, plan_name. Group them in the response: {payers: [{payer_name, plans: [...]}]}.

GET /plan-info/{plan_id} — return all columns from plans table for given plan_id or 404.

GET /providers/{ccn}/surgeons — query param cpt_code required. Join: affiliations a ON a.npi = p.npi, providers p, surgeon_volume sv ON sv.npi=p.npi AND sv.cpt_code=?, hospital_quality (not needed here). Compute years_in_practice = current_year - EXTRACT(year FROM p.npi_enum_date). Compute top_performer: true if sv.annual_volume > 75th percentile of annual_volume for the given cpt_code across all surgeons in affiliations for this CCN's state. Return list of surgeon cards sorted by annual_volume DESC: [{npi, name, credentials, specialty, annual_volume, years_in_practice, top_performer, affiliation_source}].

POST /rank-providers — body: {cpt_code, plan_id, zip, radius_miles, deductible_remaining (nullable), oop_max_remaining (nullable), cost_weight=0.6, quality_weight=0.4, sort_by='best_value', limit=6, offset=0}. Logic: (1) resolve zip to lat/lng from zipcodes table, (2) find providers within radius using PostGIS: SELECT p.npi, p.name, h.ccn, h.name as hospital_name, h.cms_star_rating, h.rand_multiplier, ST_Distance(h.geom, ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(?,?),4326)::geography)/1609.34 as distance_miles, r.rate as negotiated_rate, hq.score as comp_score FROM hospitals h JOIN affiliations a ON a.ccn=h.ccn JOIN providers p ON p.npi=a.npi JOIN rates r ON r.npi=p.npi AND r.cpt_code=? LEFT JOIN hospital_quality hq ON hq.ccn=h.ccn AND hq.measure_id='COMP_HIP_KNEE' WHERE ST_DWithin(h.geom, ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(?,?),4326)::geography, ?*1609.34) AND r.plan_id=? LIMIT 200. (3) For each result, call compute_episode_oop to get total_episode_oop. (4) Build quality_score 0-100 from cms_star_rating (35% weight) + comp_score inverted (25%) + placeholder 40%. (5) Compute rank_score = cost_weight*(1-cost_percentile) + quality_weight*quality_percentile, round to nearest 0.025. (6) Sort by sort_by param (best_value|lowest_cost|top_rated|nearest). (7) Return {total_count, providers: paginated slice}.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/main.py and backend/db.py exist in /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/
GET /health returns 200 with db status
GET /zipcodes/{zip} returns lat/lng for valid zip, 404 for unknown
GET /plans returns plans grouped by payer
GET /providers/{ccn}/surgeons returns surgeon cards sorted by volume desc
POST /rank-providers returns paginated provider list with estimated_oop and rank_score
rank_score is rounded to nearest 0.025 (e.g. 0.875 not 0.88)
CORS allows all origins — frontend at localhost:5173 can call the API

userStories.title: Build Header and Hero components
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/frontend/src/components/Header.jsx. CSS: fixed top, width 100%, height 56px, background var(--surface), border-bottom 1px solid var(--border), z-index 100. Inner layout: flexbox, align-items center, justify-content space-between, padding 0 24px. Left side: AnaCare wordmark — span 'Ana' font-weight 600 + span 'Care' font-weight 300, font-size 20px, letter-spacing -0.5px. Center: div with user name prop (default 'Joe Schmoe'), font-size 13px, font-weight 300, color var(--text-2). Right: plan name badge — span with background var(--surface-2), border 1px solid var(--border), border-radius 20px, padding 4px 12px, font-size 12px, font-weight 400. Props: userName (string, default 'Joe Schmoe'), planName (string, default null — hide badge if null).

Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/frontend/src/components/Hero.jsx. CSS: padding-top 88px (clears fixed header), padding-bottom 48px, text-align center, background radial-gradient(ellipse at 50% 0%, rgba(94,106,210,0.10) 0%, transparent 60%). Contents: (1) breadcrumb div — font-size 11px, letter-spacing 0.08em, text-transform uppercase, color var(--text-3), text 'AnaCare  ›  Compare Providers & Costs  ›  Discover care options and estimate your out-of-pocket costs before you choose'. (2) h1 'Compare real prices, estimate your cost, and find the best provider.' — font-size 40px desktop / 28px mobile via @media(max-width:768px), font-weight 300, letter-spacing -1px, max-width 660px, margin 20px auto 12px. (3) p 'Powered by CMS negotiated rate data. Real negotiated rates. Not estimates.' — font-size 14px, color var(--text-2), font-weight 300.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/Header.jsx renders AnaCare wordmark with 'Ana' bold and 'Care' light
Header is fixed at top, 56px height, dark with 1px bottom border
Plan badge only shows when planName prop is non-null
src/components/Hero.jsx renders breadcrumb, h1 headline, and subline
h1 is 40px on desktop and 28px on mobile via CSS media query
Hero has subtle radial purple gradient — no image
Both components use only CSS (zero external style libraries)

userStories.title: Build procedure selector with desktop hover panel and mobile accordion
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/frontend/src/components/ProcedureSelector.jsx.

Define at top of file (not in component): const PROCEDURE_CATEGORIES = [ {id:'orthopedic', label:'Orthopedic', procedures:[{name:'Knee Replacement',cpt:'27447'},{name:'Hip Replacement',cpt:'27130'},{name:'ACL Reconstruction',cpt:'27407'},{name:'Rotator Cuff Repair',cpt:'23412'}]}, {id:'spine',label:'Spine',procedures:[{name:'Lumbar Discectomy',cpt:'63030'}]}, {id:'gi',label:'GI',procedures:[{name:'Colonoscopy',cpt:'45378'},{name:'Cholecystectomy',cpt:'47562'}]}, {id:'cardiac',label:'Cardiac',procedures:[{name:'Cardiac Catheterization',cpt:'93455'}]}, {id:'womens_health',label:"Women's Health",procedures:[{name:'Hysterectomy',cpt:'58150'}]}, {id:'imaging',label:'Imaging',procedures:[{name:'MRI — Knee',cpt:'73721'}]} ].

Desktop layout (>768px): wrapper div position relative. Left panel 200px wide: list of category rows, each 40px tall, padding 0 16px, cursor pointer. On mouseenter a row set hoveredCategory state. On mouseleave the ENTIRE wrapper (not just the row) clear hoveredCategory. Right panel: position absolute, left 200px, top 0, width 280px, background var(--surface), border 1px solid var(--border), border-radius var(--radius-lg). Show/hide with opacity 0/1 + translateX(8px/0) CSS transition 180ms ease. When visible, list procedures for hoveredCategory — each row 36px tall, padding 0 16px, cursor pointer, flex with checkmark on right. Clicking a procedure row toggles it in selectedProcedures state array.

Mobile layout (<=768px): single column. Each category is a row that toggles open/closed on click (openCategory state). When open, show procedures in a sub-list with max-height 0 -> auto via CSS transition. Clicking a procedure toggles selection.

Below the panel (both modes): if selectedProcedures.length > 0, render a chip bar: flex-wrap, gap 8px, margin-top 16px. Each chip: '{name} — CPT {cpt}' with an × button. Font-size 12px, background var(--surface-2), border 1px solid var(--border), border-radius 20px, padding 4px 10px. Clicking × removes from selection.

Props: onChange(selectedProcedures) called on every selection change.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/ProcedureSelector.jsx exists
Desktop: hovering category shows right panel with smooth CSS transition; moving cursor off wrapper hides panel
Mobile (<768px): tapping category expands it with max-height CSS accordion; tapping another collapses first
Selecting a procedure adds it to the chip bar at bottom with × button
Clicking × removes the chip and deselects the procedure
onChange fires with current selection array on every change
No external UI libraries used

userStories.title: Build location permission modal and radius selector
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/frontend/src/components/LocationInput.jsx.

On mount, show a centered modal overlay (position fixed, inset 0, background rgba(0,0,0,0.7), z-index 200). Modal card: background var(--surface), border 1px solid var(--border), border-radius var(--radius-lg), padding 32px, max-width 400px, text-align center. Content: SVG pin icon (inline, 32px, color var(--accent)), h3 'Find providers near you' weight 500, p 'Allow location access or enter your zip code below' color var(--text-2) font-size 14px. Two buttons side by side: 'Use My Location' (background var(--accent), color white, border-radius var(--radius), padding 10px 20px) and 'Enter Zip Code' (background transparent, border 1px solid var(--border), same sizing).

If 'Use My Location' clicked: call navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition. On success: store {lat, lng} in state, close modal, show location indicator 'Using your location' with green dot. On error or permission denied: show zip code input without error message (silently fall back).

If 'Enter Zip Code' clicked or geolocation failed: close modal, show input field labeled 'ZIP Code', placeholder '60601', maxLength 5. On 5-digit input, call apiFetch('/zipcodes/' + zip) to resolve lat/lng. If 404 (unknown zip): show inline error 'ZIP code not found'. On success: store {lat, lng, zip} in state, show 'Showing providers near {city}, {state}'.

Below location indicator: radius select element with options: 10 (label '10 miles'), 25 (label '25 miles', selected by default), 50 (label '50 miles'), 100 (label '100 miles'), 0 (label 'Any distance'). Style: background var(--surface-2), border 1px solid var(--border), color var(--text), border-radius var(--radius), padding 6px 10px, appearance none.

Props: onChange({lat, lng, zip, radius_miles}) called whenever location or radius changes.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/LocationInput.jsx shows modal on mount
Successful geolocation stores lat/lng and closes modal showing green dot indicator
Geolocation failure silently falls back to zip input (no error shown)
Zip input validates 5 digits; unknown zip shows inline error
Radius dropdown defaults to 25; 'Any distance' option passes radius_miles=0 to onChange
onChange fires with lat, lng, zip, and radius_miles on any change

userStories.title: Build plan selector with dropdown, plan info modal, and deductible inputs
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/frontend/src/components/PlanSelector.jsx.

On mount, call apiFetch('/plans') and store grouped plan data in state. Render:

1. Plan dropdown row: label 'Insurance Plan', then <select> populated with <optgroup label={payer}> for each payer group, <option value={plan_id}>{plan_name} ({metal_tier}) - {network_type}</option>. Show '(Individual)' or '(Family)' if the plan name doesn't already include it. On change, store selected plan_id and call apiFetch('/plan-info/' + plan_id) to fetch full plan details. Immediately to the right of the select: a small circular button '?' (16px, background var(--surface-2), border 1px solid var(--border)) that opens the plan info modal on click.

2. Plan info modal: when open, render a fixed overlay with a centered card (max-width 480px). Header: plan_name + metal_tier badge. Body: two-column table with rows for Deductible Individual, Deductible Family, OOP Max Individual, OOP Max Family, Coinsurance (show as '20%' if 0.20 stored), PC Copay, Specialist Copay, ER Copay, Urgent Care Copay, Rx Tier 1/2/3. Show 'Not available' in var(--text-3) for NULL values. Close on backdrop click or × button in top-right.

3. Deductible Remaining row: label 'Deductible Remaining', input type=text, placeholder='$0.00'. Small '?' button that opens a modal with text: 'How to find your deductible remaining: 1. Log in to your insurance member portal (e.g. aetna.com). 2. Go to My Plan or Benefits. 3. Click Deductible & Out-of-Pocket Summary. 4. Your remaining deductible is labeled In-Network Deductible Remaining.'

4. OOP Max Remaining row: same pattern. Modal text: 'How to find your out-of-pocket remaining: Follow the same steps above. Look for Out-of-Pocket Maximum Remaining or Accumulator Summary.'

Props: onChange({plan_id, plan, deductible_remaining, oop_max_remaining}) where plan is the full plan object from /plan-info, deductible_remaining and oop_max_remaining are floats or null if blank.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/PlanSelector.jsx fetches /plans on mount and populates dropdown grouped by payer
Selecting a plan fetches /plan-info/{plan_id} and stores full plan object
Plan info modal shows all plan details in a table; NULL fields show 'Not available'
Deductible ? modal shows 4-step portal instructions
OOP ? modal shows portal instructions
onChange fires with plan_id, plan object, deductible_remaining (float or null), oop_max_remaining (float or null)

userStories.title: Build HomePage assembling the 3-step search flow
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/frontend/src/pages/HomePage.jsx. This page is the entry point — it renders the full search form.

Layout: <Header planName={plan?.payer} /> fixed at top, then <Hero /> below, then the 3-step cards section with max-width 720px, margin 0 auto, padding 0 24px 80px.

Each step is a card: background var(--surface), border 1px solid var(--border), border-radius var(--radius-lg), padding 24px, margin-bottom 16px. Top of each card: step number badge (circle 24px, background var(--accent), color white, font-size 12px font-weight 600) + step heading h3 font-size 16px font-weight 500 margin-left 10px.

Step 1 heading: 'Select a Procedure'. Contains <ProcedureSelector onChange={v => setState(s => ({...s, procedures: v}))} />.

Step 2 heading: 'Your Location'. Contains <LocationInput onChange={v => setState(s => ({...s, location: v}))} />.

Step 3 heading: 'Your Insurance Plan'. Contains <PlanSelector onChange={v => setState(s => ({...s, plan: v}))} />.

Below the cards: a full-width button 'Find Providers →' — height 52px, background var(--accent), color white, font-size 16px, font-weight 500, border-radius var(--radius), border none, cursor pointer, transition background 150ms. Disabled state (when procedures.length === 0 OR location.lat is null): background var(--surface-2), color var(--text-3), cursor not-allowed. On click when enabled: navigate('/results', {state: {procedures, location, plan}}) using useNavigate.

All step state lives in a single useState object: { procedures: [], location: {lat:null,lng:null,zip:null,radius_miles:25}, plan: {plan_id:null, plan:null, deductible_remaining:null, oop_max_remaining:null} }.
acceptanceCriteria: src/pages/HomePage.jsx renders Header, Hero, and 3 step cards
Step cards have numbered badges and headings
Find Providers button is visually disabled (grey) when no procedure selected or no location set
Clicking Find Providers when enabled navigates to /results with state: {procedures, location, plan}
Header planName prop updates live when user selects a plan
No console errors on initial page load

userStories.title: Build provider results page with sort tabs, quality slider, and provider cards
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/frontend/src/pages/ResultsPage.jsx. Read search state from useLocation().state. On mount, call POST /rank-providers with those params.

Layout: <Header planName={state.plan?.plan?.payer} /> fixed at top. Below header, results container with max-width 1100px, margin 0 auto, padding 80px 24px 48px.

Top bar: left side shows 'X providers found' (from total_count). Right side shows 'Showing top N of X · Sorted by {sort label}'.

Sort tabs: flex row gap 8px, margin-bottom 16px. Four pill buttons: Best Value, Lowest Cost, Top Rated, Nearest. Active: background var(--accent) color white. Inactive: background var(--surface-2) color var(--text-2), hover background var(--surface). On click: set sortBy state, reset visibleCount to 6, re-call API.

Quality slider (only visible when sortBy === 'best_value'): row with label 'Prioritize Cost', range input (min=0 max=100 step=5 defaultValue=60), label 'Prioritize Quality'. On change, debounce 400ms then re-call API with cost_weight=value/100, quality_weight=1-value/100.

Provider cards: CSS Grid, grid-template-columns: 1fr on mobile, repeat(2, 1fr) on >=900px, gap 16px. Each card: background var(--surface), border 1px solid var(--border), border-radius var(--radius-lg), padding 20px. Card contents: (row 1) hospital name font-weight 500 + star rating (filled ★ in var(--yellow) for cms_star_rating, e.g. 4 filled + 1 empty for rating 4). (row 2) distance badge 'X.X mi' in var(--text-3) font-size 12px. (row 3) 'Estimated Out-of-Pocket' label small + '$X,XXX' font-size 24px font-weight 500 color var(--green). (row 4) 'Negotiated Rate: $XX,XXX' font-size 13px color var(--text-2). (row 5) quality bar: label 'Quality Score' left + score right (e.g. '87.5'), bar is a div 100% wide background var(--border) height 4px border-radius 2px, filled portion width=score% background color: var(--green) if >=70, var(--yellow) if >=40, var(--red) if <40. (row 6) volume badge pill + sort tag chip (e.g. 'Best Value · #1'). (row 7) 'View Details →' button full-width, background var(--surface-2), border 1px solid var(--border).

Loading state: show 6 skeleton cards with pulse CSS animation (background linear-gradient animate). Progressive disclosure: 'Show more options' link below grid — clicking adds 3 to visibleCount. Switching sort tab resets visibleCount to 6.
acceptanceCriteria: src/pages/ResultsPage.jsx calls POST /rank-providers on mount using router state params
Sort tabs update sortBy and reset visible cards to 6 on click
Quality slider only shows on Best Value tab and debounces API re-call at 400ms
Provider cards render all required fields: hospital name, stars, distance, OOP, negotiated rate, quality bar, volume, sort tag
Quality bar color changes: green >=70, yellow >=40, red <40
Progressive disclosure adds exactly 3 cards per click
Skeleton loading cards show while API is fetching

userStories.title: Build hospital detail drawer with 3-phase cost timeline
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/frontend/src/components/HospitalDrawer.jsx. Triggered by 'View Details' on a provider card — receives props: ccn, hospitalName, cptCode, planId, deductibleRemaining, oopMaxRemaining, isOpen, onClose.

CSS: position fixed, top 0, right 0, height 100vh, width 520px (100% on mobile <600px), background var(--surface), border-left 1px solid var(--border), z-index 300, overflow-y auto, transform translateX(100%) when !isOpen, translateX(0) when isOpen, transition transform 260ms ease. Backdrop: position fixed, inset 0, background rgba(0,0,0,0.5), z-index 299, only rendered when isOpen, clicking closes drawer.

On isOpen change to true: fetch GET /providers/{ccn}/episode?cpt_code={cptCode}&plan_id={planId}&deductible={deductibleRemaining}&oop_max={oopMaxRemaining}. Add this endpoint to backend/main.py: it runs compute_episode_oop for the given hospital's negotiated rate and plan, returns the full episode OOP breakdown dict. Also fetch comp_risk from hospital_quality WHERE ccn=ccn AND measure_id='COMP_HIP_KNEE'.

Drawer contents:
- Top bar: hospital name h2 font-weight 500 + × close button top-right.
- Total OOP: label 'Your Estimated Episode Cost', amount '$X,XXX' font-size 32px font-weight 300 color var(--green).
- 'How we calculated this' toggle (chevron): expands the 3-phase timeline section.
- Phase sections (when expanded): each phase has a colored left border (blue pre-op, purple surgery, green post-op), heading 'Phase 1 — Before Surgery' etc., and a table with columns: Item | CPT | Qty | Gross | Your Cost. Use <table> with thead. Phase total row: bold, 'Phase Total' | — | — | $X,XXX | $XXX. Apply alternating row background var(--surface-2) for readability.
- Complication Risk section: row showing 'Complication rate: X.X% at this hospital vs X.X% national avg'. Toggle buttons [Base estimate] [+ Complication risk] — switching to complication adds a row to Phase 3 table.
- <details> sections: 'What's included in this estimate' and 'Not included in this estimate' (list items from PRD section 9.4).
- Disclaimer: italic font-size 11px color var(--text-3): 'Cost estimates are based on CMS negotiated rate data and your plan's cost-sharing rules. Actual costs may vary. Not a guarantee of payment.'
- <SurgeonCards ccn={ccn} cptCode={cptCode} /> component rendered after disclaimer.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/HospitalDrawer.jsx slides in from right with CSS transform transition
Fetches episode OOP breakdown from GET /providers/{ccn}/episode on open
All 3 phase tables render with item rows (name, CPT, qty, gross, your cost)
Phase total rows show correctly
Complication toggle switches between base and complication-adjusted estimates
What's included / Not included sections use HTML <details> elements
Disclaimer text renders at bottom of cost section
GET /providers/{ccn}/episode endpoint is added to backend/main.py

userStories.title: Build surgeon cards component inside hospital drawer
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/frontend/src/components/SurgeonCards.jsx. Props: ccn, cptCode.

On mount: call apiFetch('/providers/' + ccn + '/surgeons?cpt_code=' + cptCode). While loading: show 2 skeleton cards (same pulse animation as results page). On error: show 'Surgeon data unavailable' in var(--text-3).

Section heading: 'Surgeons at this hospital' font-size 14px font-weight 500 color var(--text-2) text-transform uppercase letter-spacing 0.06em, margin-bottom 12px.

Each surgeon card (flex row, background var(--surface-2), border 1px solid var(--border), border-radius var(--radius), padding 14px 16px, margin-bottom 8px):
- Left: initials avatar — circle 36px background var(--surface), border 1px solid var(--border), font-size 13px font-weight 500, centered initials from first+last name.
- Right column (flex 1): top row: name + credentials 'Dr. Jane Smith, MD' font-weight 500 font-size 14px. Second row: specialty font-size 12px color var(--text-2). Third row: flex gap 8px — volume pill ('{N}/year' background var(--surface) border 1px solid var(--border) border-radius 20px padding 2px 8px font-size 11px; color var(--green) if top_performer else var(--text-2)) + 'Top Performer' pill (background rgba(94,106,210,0.15) color var(--accent) same sizing, only shown if top_performer). Fourth row: 'In practice since {year} ({N} years)' font-size 11px color var(--text-3).
- If affiliation_source === 'city_state_fallback': show below the card a small italic note 'Affiliated via location match' font-size 11px color var(--text-3).

If surgeons array is empty: show 'No surgeon data available for this procedure at this hospital.' padding 16px color var(--text-3).

If surgeons.length > 4: show first 4, then a 'Show all {N} surgeons' text link (color var(--accent), font-size 13px) that expands the list.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/SurgeonCards.jsx fetches /providers/{ccn}/surgeons on mount
Each card shows initials avatar, name+credentials, specialty, volume pill, years in practice
'Top Performer' pill is shown only for top_performer=true surgeons
'Affiliated via location match' italic note appears only for city_state_fallback source
Empty state message shows when no surgeons returned
More than 4 surgeons shows first 4 with 'Show all N' expand link

userStories.title: Seed demo data and run end-to-end integration smoke test
userStories.description:
Create /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/tools/seed_demo_data.py. This script inserts realistic demo data into a local PostgreSQL instance for development and demo purposes when real MRF data is not yet ingested. Insert:

5 hospitals in Chicago IL (60601-60640 zip range): Northwestern Memorial Hospital, Rush University Medical Center, University of Chicago Medicine, NorthShore University HealthSystem, Advocate Illinois Masonic. Use real CCNs if known (Northwestern=140281, Rush=140119, UChicago=140088) or placeholder CCNs. Set cms_star_rating for each (4-5 stars), rand_multiplier between 2.2 and 3.1. Insert lat/lng from approximate coordinates.

10 surgeon providers affiliated with those hospitals: 2 per hospital, orthopedic specialty, taxonomy 207X00000X. Set realistic npi_enum_dates (2005-2015 range). Insert surgeon_volume for CPT 27447 with volumes 80-450/year.

3 Aetna plans: Aetna PPO Gold IL 2026 (plan_id='AETNA-PPO-GOLD-IL-2026', deductible_ind=1500, oop_max_ind=6000, coinsurance_pct=0.20, specialist_copay=50, rx_tier1=10, rx_tier2=35, rx_tier3=70), Aetna PPO Silver IL 2026 (deductible_ind=3500, oop_max_ind=8000, coinsurance_pct=0.30), Aetna HMO Bronze IL 2026 (deductible_ind=6000, oop_max_ind=8550, coinsurance_pct=0.40).

Rates for CPT 27447 for each of the 10 surgeons: negotiated rates ranging $14,000-$32,000, plan_id='AETNA-PPO-GOLD-IL-2026'.

hospital_quality rows for each hospital: COMP_HIP_KNEE scores ranging 0.015-0.042, READM_30_HOSP_WIDE scores 0.85-1.05.

After seeding, run the full user flow manually and verify: (1) backend starts with uvicorn, (2) POST /rank-providers with zip=60601, cpt_code=27447, plan_id=AETNA-PPO-GOLD-IL-2026, radius_miles=25, deductible_remaining=1400, oop_max_remaining=4600 returns 5+ providers with valid estimated_oop values, (3) GET /providers/{ccn}/surgeons?cpt_code=27447 returns 2 surgeons. Document any failures in KNOWN_GAPS.md at /Users/joshuakao/anacare_product/KNOWN_GAPS.md.
acceptanceCriteria: tools/seed_demo_data.py exists and runs without errors against a fresh local PostgreSQL instance
After seed: 5 hospitals, 10 providers, 3 plans, 10 rates, hospital_quality rows exist in DB
POST /rank-providers with demo params returns at least 3 providers with non-null estimated_oop
GET /providers/{ccn}/surgeons returns 2 surgeons for any of the 5 seeded hospitals
Frontend at localhost:5173 can complete the full flow: select procedure → set location → select plan → view results → open drawer → see cost timeline and surgeon cards without JS errors
KNOWN_GAPS.md exists and documents any unfinished integration points
